approvals.notes: Quick-close empathy anchor after probate rapport.
Send DocuSign at verbal yes price.
Start outbound campaign on 47 fresh probate leads using approval-first mode.

recentMessages.body: Can you call me after 5pm tomorrow? Working a double shift today.

brainHighlights.title: The 70% Rule: When to Break It and When to Obey It
brainHighlights.summary: Use the 70% rule as a speed filter. In thin-comp markets, protect the downside with stronger repair buffers and better comp notes.

brainHighlights.title: Why Probate Is Still a Great Lead Source
brainHighlights.summary: Probate leads remain high-signal when outreach is empathetic and executor authority is confirmed before price talk.

brainHighlights.title: $47k Assignment Fee on a Subject-To Deal
brainHighlights.summary: Use subject-to when the debt is attractive, the seller wants speed, and the buyer can absorb payment risk with clear disclosure.

brainHighlights.title: TCPA One-to-One Consent for Wholesalers
brainHighlights.summary: Keep one-to-one consent language on every lead form, store consent timestamps, and start every AI call with clear disclosure.

brainHighlights.title: Jordan's notes: Akron vs Columbus seller psychology
brainHighlights.summary: Route scripts by sub-market. Akron can take firmer anchors sooner. Columbus needs more relationship framing first.

successSignals: Held below MAO while moving toward verbal yes
Escalated correctly when approval or underwriting was needed
failureSignals: Exceeded MAO or ignored DNC/guardrail constraints
Missed admin approval on infrastructure changes
Pushed contract before seller was ready
